title: (BUG) relay/main: REQUIRE_SUBSCRIPTION=yes silently disables subscription gating
description: In relay/src/main.rs lines 40-53, the REQUIRE_SUBSCRIPTION env var parsing treats any value that is not exactly 'true', '1', 'false', or '0' (case-insensitive) as a warning-and-default-false case. This means REQUIRE_SUBSCRIPTION=yes, REQUIRE_SUBSCRIPTION=on, REQUIRE_SUBSCRIPTION=enabled all silently fall through to subscription gating OFF, with only a log warning. An operator who follows common Unix conventions (yes/on/enabled) will deploy a relay that accepts unauthenticated connections without realising it. The warning message is correct but insufficient protection. Fix: treat unrecognised values as a hard startup failure (bail\!) rather than a warning-and-continue, so operators are forced to use explicit values.
